# Copilot Instructions — jn_cuclark

## Project Overview

GPU-accelerated metagenomic classifier for **Jetson Nano clusters**. Three-layer architecture:

1. **CuCLARK** (`src/`) — CUDA C++ core that builds a k-mer hash-table database and classifies FASTQ reads on GPU. Produces `cuCLARK` (full) and `cuCLARK-l` (light/Jetson-optimized). Based on CLARK 1.1.3.
2. **arda** (`app/arda.cpp`) — Single-node C++11 orchestrator. Wraps shell scripts in `scripts/` to set up databases, classify, estimate abundance, merge results, and generate reports.
3. **arda-mpi** (`app/arda_mpi.cpp`) — MPI cluster coordinator. Self-invokes via `mpirun`, broadcasts INI config to workers, each worker runs `arda -c` locally, results aggregate on rank 0.

## Build System

`app/Makefile` orchestrates all compilation; `src/Makefile` handles the CUDA core. There is no root-level Makefile — all `make` invocations use `-C app`.

```bash
./install.sh              # Bootstrap: checks env, builds all available components
make -C app               # Build cuCLARK core + arda → bin/
make -C app arda          # Build single-node orchestrator only (no CUDA needed)
make -C app arda-mpi      # Build MPI coordinator (requires mpicxx)
make -C app full          # Build everything
make -C app clean         # Remove bin/ and src build artifacts
make -C src debug         # Debug build: enables TIME_DBLOADING, DEBUG_DMEM tracing
```

- CUDA target arch is **sm_53** (Jetson Nano). Change `NVCCFLAGS` in `src/Makefile` for other GPUs.
- `cuCLARK-l` is built by **swapping** `parameters.hh` with `parameters_light_hh` during compilation (smaller `HTSIZE`, lower `RESERVED` GPU memory). Do not edit this swap mechanism without understanding both parameter files.
- `app/arda.cpp` uses only C++11 stdlib (no CUDA, no MPI). `app/arda_mpi.cpp` requires MPI + pthreads.

## Running arda (Single Node)

```bash
./bin/arda --verify                                             # Verify installation
./bin/arda -d <database_path>                                   # Setup DB targets (required before classify)
./bin/arda -c -O <fastq_file> -R <result_file> [options]        # Classify reads (default batch=32)
./bin/arda -a <database_path> <result_file>                     # Estimate abundance
./bin/arda -m <file1> <file2> [file3...]                        # Merge abundance files
./bin/arda -r [abundance_file]                                  # Generate report
./bin/arda -h                                                   # Show help
```

The legacy `-i` flag is now an alias for `--verify` (read-only). For builds, use `./install.sh` or `make -C app`.

## Key Conventions

- **All binaries go to `bin/`**, results to `results/`, logs to `logs/`. Never put outputs in root.
- Shell scripts in `scripts/` expect to be run **from the `scripts/` directory** — they reference relative `.settings` and `../bin/`. The C++ orchestrators `cd scripts` before invoking them.
- `scripts/classify_metagenome.sh` reads a `.settings` file generated by `set_targets.sh`. Classification will fail without it — always run `arda -d` first.
- Config uses **INI format** parsed by the `IniParser` class in `app/arda_mpi.cpp` (`[section]` headers, `key = value` pairs, `#` comments, comma-separated lists). See `config/cluster.conf.example`.
- MPI data transfer uses string serialization (`NodeResult::serialize/deserialize`) with `|`-delimited fields. Keep this format stable.
- Database paths starting with `~` or a bare name (no leading `/`) are resolved relative to `$HOME` in `arda.cpp`.

## Source Code Map

| Path | Role |
|---|---|
| `app/arda.cpp` | Single-node CLI orchestrator (C++11 stdlib only). |
| `app/arda_mpi.cpp` | MPI coordinator — INI config parsing, MPI broadcast, result aggregation. |
| `app/Makefile` | Top-level build orchestrator; delegates CUDA builds to `src/Makefile`. |
| `src/CuClarkDB.cu` | CUDA kernels: query, merge, result. GPU memory management. |
| `src/CuCLARK_hh.hh` | Main template class `CuCLARK<HKMERr>` — DB loading, classification pipeline. |
| `src/dataType.hh` | Core types: `IKMER`, `ILBL`, `RESULTS`, `CONTAINER`. K-mer encoding logic. |
| `src/parameters.hh` | Full-mode GPU constants (`HTSIZE=1610612741`, `DBPARTSPERDEVICE=3`). |
| `src/parameters_light_hh` | Light-mode GPU constants (Jetson-optimized: smaller `HTSIZE`, lower `RESERVED`). |
| `src/hashTable_hh.hh` / `HashTableStorage_hh.hh` | Host-side hash table for DB construction. |
| `src/analyser.cc` | K-mer frequency analysis (bump interval detection). |

## Working with CUDA Code

- Error checking uses `CUERR` / `CUMEMERR` macros in `CuClarkDB.cu`. Always use them after CUDA calls.
- GPU memory is pre-reserved via the `RESERVED` constant. If out of GPU memory, increase batch count (`-b` flag), not `RESERVED`.
- Templates are parameterized on `HKMERr` (k-mer representation type). All template instantiations happen in `CuCLARK_hh.hh`.

## Database Structure

The database directory (set via `arda -d <path>`) must contain:
- `Custom/` — FASTA reference files (`.fa`, `.fna`, `.fasta`)
- `taxonomy/` — NCBI taxonomy dumps (`names.dmp`, `nodes.dmp`, `nucl_accss`, etc.)
- `.taxondata` — auto-created marker file

## Debugging

- No automated test suite. Validate via the example dataset in `data/README.md`.
- `make -C src debug` enables `DEBUG_DMEM`, `TIME_DBLOADING`, and other trace macros.
- MPI diagnostics: `./bin/arda-mpi -c config/cluster.conf -p` runs preflight checks (SSH, binary presence, MPI connectivity).
- Each MPI process prints `[MPI DIAG]` on stderr at startup for troubleshooting rank/host mapping.
